sections.paragraph:
to languish, to faint; comp. the cognate roots עוּף (עָלַף, עָטַף), יָעַף. Once found as a verb, Jer 4:31. Hence-

עיף

f. עֲיֵפָה adj. languishing, especially used of one who is wearied out, either with a journey or with toil, and at the same time suffers from thirst; see especially Gen 25:29, 30 Gen 25:30 Job 22:7 (in the other hemist. רָעֵב); Psa 63:2. Pro 25:25, “cold waters to a languishing (i.e. thirsty) soul.” Jer 31:25, “I will give drink to the thirsty.” It is used also of cattle when wearied, Isa 46:1 (where עֲיֵפָה is neutr. wearied, fessum, i.e. wearied beasts, i.q. חַיָּה עֲיֵפָה); used of a thirsty land, Psa 143:6 Isa 32:2.
